title: Tuples and sets - fixed, and unique
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A tuple is an ordered group that can't be changed. A set is an unordered collection with no duplicates.
explanation: Use a tuple when a group belongs together and shouldn't shift - (x, y) coordinates, a row. Use a set when you only care whether something is in it, and want duplicates gone automatically. set([1, 2, 2, 3]) is {1, 2, 3}. Asking 'is this in the set' is fast.
code_example:
seen = set()
for c in "banana":
    seen.add(c)
print(len(seen))

quiz.question: How many unique letters are in 'banana'?

hints: A set drops repeats. Which distinct letters appear?
The letters used are b, a, n.
